title: Tiny Kitchen Prep Cart with Trash Bin
summary: A genuinely small rolling prep cart with a hinged door hiding a built-in trash bin and a slatted lower shelf — a compact plywood-and-2x2 build sized for tight kitchens.
description:
This cart is deliberately small and rolling, sized to hide a trash bin behind a hinged door while still offering usable prep counter space and a bottom shelf — a genuinely tight-footprint alternative to a full kitchen island. Build the two end frames first from 2x2s and plywood side panels — the plan notes joining the 1x2 trim to the 2x2 legs isn't structurally required, it just keeps that joint snug, so you could attach the plywood first and nail the trim on after if that's easier for your process.

**If you're using a hinged, hinge-down drawer or door face and don't own a right-angle drill, attach it before final assembly** — the plan flags this explicitly, since certain hinge and hardware positions become very hard to reach with a standard drill once the cart is fully boxed together. Attach the plywood side panels to the legs with pocket holes, tying the whole side assembly together with the 1x2 trim and glue at every joint.

Build any doors or drawers to fit the actual opening, leaving an 1/8" gap around all sides as a default, but **measure your actual hinges first** — the plan notes simple butt hinges can eat up closer to 1/4" of that gap, so the door dimensions need to account for the specific hardware you're using rather than a blanket gap number. Caster wheels on the base finish the piece, making it a genuinely mobile trash-and-prep station rather than a fixed cabinet.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood or MDF project panel, 24 in x 48 in
Material for door/drawer, 1x3 frame and 1/4" plywood panel
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" plywood or MDF project panel, 24 in x 48 in
3/4" plywood or MDF project panel, 24 in x 48 in

cutList.part: Bottom shelf outside trim, panel sides
Bottom shelf outside trim, drawer sides

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 32-3/4" and four tie-in trim at 16-1/2" from the 2x2. Cut four side trim at 15-3/4", one door/drawer trim at 16-1/2", two bottom shelf outside trim, panel sides at 15-3/4", two bottom shelf outside trim, drawer sides at 16-1/2" and four bottom shelf slats at 18" from the 1x2. Cut two side panels at 24" and one bottom at 16-1/2" from the 3/4" plywood or MDF project panel, 24 in x 48 in. Cut one top drawer at 16-1/4" from the 1x6. Cut six tabletop boards at 20-1/4" from the 1x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to three 8 ft 2x2, three 8 ft 1x2 and one 12 ft 1x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.
materials: 3/4" plywood or MDF project panel, 24 in x 48 in

steps.title: Build the end frames
steps.body: Build two end frames from the legs and side trim. Joining the trim to the legs first isn't structurally required, just keeps the joint snug — attach the plywood first if you'd rather nail the trim on after.

steps.title: Attach the side panels
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on the sides and tops of the plywood side panels on the inside. Attach to the 2x2 legs, and nail and glue to the 1x2 trim pieces.
materials: 3/4" plywood or MDF project panel, 24 in x 48 in

steps.title: Attach a hinge-down door face now if needed
steps.body: If using a hinge-down drawer face and you don't have a right-angle drill, attach it now before further assembly.
materials: Material for door/drawer, 1x3 frame and 1/4" plywood panel

steps.title: Tie the sides together
steps.body: Attach trim pieces to the side panels to tie the project together, using glue at all joints.

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around all four sides of the bottom plywood piece. Attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue.
materials: 3/4" plywood or MDF project panel, 24 in x 48 in

steps.title: Build the doors and drawer
steps.body: Build doors and drawers to fit the actual openings, leaving an 1/8" gap around all sides as a starting point but measuring your actual hinges first, since butt hinges can require closer to 1/4".
materials: Material for door/drawer, 1x3 frame and 1/4" plywood panel

steps.title: Add the tabletop and shelf slats
steps.body: Attach the tabletop boards on top, and the bottom shelf slats and trim below.

steps.title: Install hardware, casters, and finish
steps.body: Attach knobs and pulls to doors/drawer. Attach caster wheels to the base. Sand and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Tiny rolling kitchen prep cart with a hidden trash bin
